food_name: Seafood Hor Fun (Stir-Fried Flat Rice Noodles with Prawns, Squid and Fish Cake in Egg Gravy)
region_of_origin: Singapore (Cantonese origin, adapted as Singapore hawker and zi char dish)
food_subgroup: Stir-fried flat rice noodles in egg gravy with mixed seafood (prawns, squid, fish cake); reference serving 1 plate (~450g)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Seafood Hor Fun. Closest parent is a generic flat rice noodle dish concept. No code assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle dish — rice noodles, stir-fried with seafood and sauce (closest FoodEx2 parent: composite rice noodle dish)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GJM used as closest defensible parent for composite rice noodle dish with seafood. No exact FoodEx2 entry for Seafood Hor Fun exists in the EFSA matrix.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Seafood Hor Fun or a sufficiently close parent concept in the clinical terminology.

main_ingredients: flat rice noodles (kway teow / hor fun)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Seafood Hor Fun weighs approximately 450g, comprising flat rice noodles, mixed seafood (prawns, squid, fish cake), and egg gravy, as commonly served at Singapore hawker centres and zi char restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Seafood Hor Fun — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB / Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Seafood Hor Fun (stir-fried flat rice noodles with seafood in egg gravy). HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker and zi char dishes. All per-100g values are taken from this verified entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — flat rice noodles (hor fun / kway teow) analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Seafood Hor Fun as a composite dish. GI ~60 (medium) is estimated from flat rice noodles (hor fun / kway teow), which have a published GI of approximately 60–65 in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008). The egg gravy (cornstarch-thickened) and seafood components do not materially alter the GI of the noodle base. GL per 450g serving = (GI × carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 49.5) / 100 = 29.7, classified as high GL. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 155mg/100g — approximately 698mg per 450g serving from oyster sauce, soy sauce, and stock
hypertension.guidance: At ~698mg sodium per plate, Seafood Hor Fun contributes approximately 35% of the 2,000mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive patients; individuals with hypertension should request reduced sauce or share the dish to limit sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic load (GL ~29.7 per 450g serving) driven by the large flat rice noodle portion (~300g) despite a medium GI of 60
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high GL per standard serving makes Seafood Hor Fun unsuitable for T2DM patients without portion modification; clinically, patients should request a half noodle portion (~225g) with extra seafood to reduce GL to approximately 15, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to further blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.5g/100g = 2.3g per serving) and zero trans fat; moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Seafood Hor Fun is low in saturated fat and trans fat, making it a relatively heart-friendly hawker option; however, the moderate sodium content (~698mg per serving) warrants attention for patients on sodium-restricted cardiac diets, and the refined carbohydrate load should be moderated in patients with concurrent dyslipidaemia.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat; mixed seafood provides moderate omega-3 and selenium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The dish is not a significant pro-inflammatory trigger; selenium from prawns and squid supports antioxidant function, and the low saturated fat content is consistent with an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern, though the refined carbohydrate base should be moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Seafood Hor Fun is a beloved Singapore zi char and hawker dish consisting of silky flat rice noodles (hor fun or kway teow) stir-fried in a wok over high heat and served bathed in a thick, flavoursome egg gravy. The gravy is made from chicken stock, oyster sauce, soy sauce, and cornstarch-thickened beaten egg poured over the noodles, creating a semi-set, glossy coating. The dish is topped with mixed seafood — typically whole prawns, rings of squid, and sliced fish cake — and is distinguished by its 'wok hei' (breath of the wok) smoky char. The texture is soft and slippery from the noodles, with a savoury, umami-rich gravy and tender seafood. Originating from Cantonese cuisine (hor fun in oyster sauce is a classic Hong Kong dim sum staple), the dish has evolved in Singapore into a standalone plate dish served at zi char restaurants and hawker centres. At 450g per standard serving, the nutritional profile per plate is: ~347 kcal, 18g protein, 8.1g fat, 49.5g carbohydrate, and ~698mg sodium. The glycaemic load is high (~29.7) despite a medium GI (~60) because of the large noodle portion. For T2DM patients, requesting a half noodle portion with extra seafood is the primary mitigation strategy. The dish is low in saturated fat (2.3g per serving) and contains no trans fat, making it relatively heart-friendly. Selenium from prawns and squid supports antioxidant function. For a lower-GL alternative, clear-broth seafood bee hoon is recommended.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the primary approved source for Singapore hawker dishes. The entry is an exact match for Seafood Hor Fun. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (flat rice noodles, University of Sydney GI Database) with documented basis. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Confidence is high.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Singapore hawker dish is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Foodo source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source — no trans fats detected in this dish). All core macros (protein_g 4.0, fat_g 1.8, carbohydrate_g 11.0) are non-zero and sourced from HPB Singapore. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate from flat rice noodles (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement exists for the composite dish. GL per 450g serving calculated as (60 × 49.5) / 100 = 29.7 (high). FoodEx2 code A0GJM used as closest defensible parent for composite rice noodle dish; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Seafood Hor Fun. Halal status not confirmed — fish cake and oyster sauce sourcing may vary by vendor; marked false pending vendor-level verification.
